title: Partial profit scaling via indicator signals
thesis_bullets: Potentially take partial profits based on an indicator rather than full in/out trade management.
Keep full-close signal capability for exits, but for scaling out, take 10% off position each time the take-profit signal fires while the position is in profit.
Repeat up to 10 times to fully close the trade.
This acts as risk mitigation and has shown decent results; need to identify robust concepts/indicators that are suitable for partial profit-taking triggers.

hypotheses.statement: BTC continuation edge appears when signal alignment persists on 1h.
hypotheses.rationale: Research and indicator mentions converge on trend-continuation logic with confirmation filters.
hypotheses.falsifiable_test: Backtest BTC on 1h: require >=55% win rate and positive expectancy over 200+ trades; reject if unmet.

candidate_signals.name: alignment_entry
candidate_signals.description: Enter only when primary trend signal and confirmation filter agree on bar close.
